query: What are the expressions of The Glenlivet?
retrieved_chunks:

response:
The Glenlivet has several expressions, including:

* The Glenlivet 18 Year Old
* The Glenlivet 19 Year Old Indulgent Chocolate
* The Glenlivet 20 Year Old Orchard Fruitiness
* The Glenlivet 25 Year Old
* The Glenlivet Cask Master’s Collection
* The Glenlivet Small Batch Collection
* The Glenlivet 56 Year Old (part of The Glenlivet Eternal Collection)

These expressions offer a range of flavors and characteristics, from fruity and syrupy to smooth and rich.

Answer:
The Glenlivet has a range of expressions with different flavor profiles.

query: What makes Jameson Irish Whiskey distinctive in its production?
retrieved_chunks:
Sources:

• Jameson
  Jameson Irish Whiskey - Wikipedia
  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jameson_whiskey

• Jameson
  Jameson Irish Whiskey
  https://www.jamesonwhiskey.com/en/
response:
Answer:
Jameson Irish Whiskey is distinctive in its production due to the following factors:
* It is produced from a blend of grain whiskey and single pot still whiskey.
* The single pot still whiskey uses a mixture of malted and unmalted or "green" Irish barley, all sourced from within a fifty-mile radius around the distillery in Cork.
* The barley is dried in a closed kiln fired by natural gas, which is in contrast to the traditional method used in some Scotch whisky distilleries that fire the kiln with peat, adding a distinctive peat flavour.

Sources:
• Brand: Jameson
• Title: Jameson Irish Whiskey
• URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jameson_Irish_Whiskey
pass_condition: Mentions triple distillation and / or blending of pot still and grain whiskey

query: Tell me about Beefeater Gin — its botanicals and origin.
retrieved_chunks:
Sources:

• Beefeater
  Beefeater Gin - Wikipedia
  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beefeater_gin

• Beefeater
  The World's Most Awarded Gin - Beefeater Gin GB
  https://www.beefeatergin.com/en-gb/
response:
Answer:
Beefeater Gin is a brand of gin that contains nine different botanicals: 
* Juniper
* Angelica root
* Angelica seeds
* Coriander seeds
* Liquorice
* Almonds
* Orris root
* Seville oranges
* Lemon peel
The gin is manufactured by James Burrough Ltd and has been in production since 1863. The original Beefeater recipe book, dated 1895, specifies that these nine botanicals are essential to create the full-bodied and robust flavor of the gin. Beefeater Gin is currently located in Kennington, London.

Sources:
• Brand: Beefeater Gin
• Title: Beefeater Gin - Wikipedia
• URL: https://www.beefeatergin.com/en-gb/

* Gin: Beefeater, Cork Dry, Inverroche, KI NO BI, Malfy, Monkey 47, Plymouth Gin, Seagram's Gin, Ungava

* Scotch Whisky: Blenders Pride, Chivas Regal, Ballantine’s, The Glenlivet, Royal Salute
* Wine: Jacob’s Creek, Orlando, St Hugo, Stoneleigh, Brancott Estate, Church Road, Campo Viejo, Ysios, Tarsus, Azpilicueta

query: When was Chivas Regal founded and what makes it distinctive?
retrieved_chunks:
Sources:

• Chivas Regal
  Chivas Regal - Wikipedia
  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chivas_Regal

• Pernod Ricard
  Pernod Ricard, créateurs de convivialité
  https://www.pernod-ricard.com/en
response:
Answer:
Chivas Regal was founded in 1909 by Chivas Brothers Master Blender Charles Stewart Howard as a 25-year-old luxury whisky. What makes Chivas Regal distinctive is its blend of quality, age, and craftsmanship, with a range of expressions including 12, 15, 18, and 25-year-old whiskies. The brand is also known for its unique collaborations, such as the Chivas Regal 12 Years Old x Gaurav Gupta edition, which features a silver aesthetic and refined design.

Sources:
• Brand: Chivas Regal
• Title: Chivas Regal
• URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chivas_Regal

query: How was Pernod Ricard formed and when?
retrieved_chunks:
Sources:

• Pernod Ricard
  Pernod Ricard - Wikipedia
  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pernod_Ricard

• Pernod Ricard
  Our History | Pernod Ricard
  https://www.pernod-ricard.com/en/our-group/our-history
response:
Pernod Ricard was formed in 1975 through the merger of two French anise-based spirits companies, Pernod and Ricard. The company traces its roots back to 1805, when Pernod was born, and 1932, when Ricard was founded by Paul Ricard. The two families joined forces in 1975, giving birth to Pernod Ricard.

Answer:
Pernod Ricard was formed in 1975 through the merger of Pernod and Ricard.

Sources:
• Brand: Pernod Ricard
• Title: Our History
• URL: pernod-ricard.com
